title: Renaissance art was a weapon - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer argues that Renaissance art served as a strategic tool or "weapon" with specific purposes beyond aesthetic creation. The discussion examines the instrumental role art played during the Renaissance period.
writeup: Historian Ada Palmer argues that Renaissance art operated as a strategic instrument of power and influence rather than a purely aesthetic pursuit. The conversation reframes the period's cultural production, treating commissioned works as tools deployed by patrons to advance political and social ends. The takeaway: understanding Renaissance art means reading it through the logic of power, not just artistic achievement.
